blocks.body: A flat surface formed by two perpendicular number lines: the horizontal x-axis and vertical y-axis, intersecting at the origin (0, 0).
A point written as (x, y) where x is the horizontal position and y is the vertical position. Example: (3, -2) means 3 right, 2 down from the origin.
The four quadrants: Quadrant I (+,+), Quadrant II (-,+), Quadrant III (-,-), Quadrant IV (+,-). Points on the axes are not in any quadrant.
To plot (x, y): start at the origin. Move right if x is positive (left if negative). Then move up if y is positive (down if negative). Mark the point.

blocks.label: Examples
blocks.body: Plot A(2,3): right 2, up 3. Plot B(-1,4): left 1, up 4. Plot C(0,-3): on y-axis, down 3.
The x-coordinate is always listed first, the y-coordinate second. (3,5) and (5,3) are different points.
